sonnet_review.reason: This aligns with my detection of the full phrase 'The decisions amount to a sweeping legal rebuke of Trump's immigration crackdown' as spin. Sonnet correctly identifies 'sweeping' as a subjective characterization.

sonnet_review.biasedText: A conservative appeals court in New Orleans last week gave the Trump administration a victory in its drive to lock up more immigrants.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged 'a conservative appeals court' as a subjective qualifying adjective. Sonnet's broader slant detection about asymmetric coverage is also well-supported — the article does give disproportionate space to rulings against the administration.

sonnet_review.biasedText: drive to lock up more immigrants
sonnet_review.reason: This is a valid detection. 'Drive to lock up' is loaded language in the author's voice that frames the administration's policy negatively. While I didn't flag this exact phrase separately, it falls under the same pattern I identified with 'jailing' and 'crackdown.'
This matches my detection of the same phrase. 'Jailing' carries stronger punitive connotations than the neutral legal term 'detaining.'

gpt_review.biasedText: The decisions amount to a sweeping legal rebuke of Trump's immigration crackdown. Yet the administration has continued jailing people indefinitely even after courts ruled the policy was illegal.
gpt_review.reason: This overlaps with two of my detections — the spin in 'sweeping legal rebuke of Trump's immigration crackdown' and the loaded word choice in 'jailing people indefinitely.' GPT correctly identifies the negative framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: Sheer scale of the lawsuits threatens to clog the judicial system
gpt_review.reason: I did not flag this, but 'sheer scale' and 'threatens to clog' are indeed more dramatic than necessary. However, this appears in the article summary/bullet points rather than the body text, and it does describe a factual situation — but the language choice is arguably sensationalized.
This matches my detection exactly. The standalone sentence is strategically placed after sympathetic cases to imply broader patterns without providing context on the overall detained population.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: as it carries out its mass-deportation campaign
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the loaded term 'campaign' applied to the administration's deportation policy in the author's voice, which frames enforcement as aggressive political action.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Peltier said they were stopped for "driving while brown."
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the inclusion of this serious racial profiling allegation without corroboration or counterpoint, which gives it prominence despite being unverified.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Within a span of a few days in January, lawyers filed habeas petitions for Liam Conejo, a five-year-old Ecuadorean boy detained in the driveway of his Minnesota home; a Ukrainian man with a valid temporary humanitarian status who was detained on his way to work as a cable technician; a Salvadoran man married to a U.S. citizen and father of a 3-year-old autistic child who is also a U.S. citizen; an Eritrean hospital worker with refugee status who was arrested after letting agents into his apartment complex and a Venezuelan man who was arrested after dropping off his daughter at school.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: While Sonnet noted the structural imbalance generally, it did not specifically identify this curated list of maximally sympathetic cases as a slant technique.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: a conservative appeals court in New Orleans
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the editorial characterization of labeling the court 'conservative' which frames the ruling through an ideological lens.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: the administration has continued jailing people indefinitely even after courts ruled the policy was illegal
gpt_false_negatives.reason: While GPT flagged this as part of a larger passage under 'Negativity Bias,' it did not specifically identify 'jailing' as loaded word choice compared to the neutral 'detaining.'

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: as it carries out its mass-deportation campaign
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the loaded term 'campaign' which adds aggressive political connotations to what could be described more neutrally as enforcement operations.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: Peltier said they were stopped for "driving while brown."
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed this unsubstantiated racial profiling allegation included without corroboration or counterpoint.
